title: Curve Fitting

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Curve fitting extracts kinetic parameters from raw fluorescence time series data. The exponential approach model fits the characteristic shape of IVT reactions.

sections.heading: The Kinetic Model
sections.content: F(t) = F_max × (1 - exp(-k_obs × (t - t_lag))) + F_0, where F_max is maximum fluorescence, k_obs is the rate constant, t_lag is lag time, and F_0 is baseline.

sections.heading: Fit Quality Indicators
sections.content: R² measures how well the model explains variance (>0.95 is good). RMSE measures average residual magnitude. Both should be inspected for systematic deviations.

subsections.heading: Good Fit
subsections.content: R² > 0.95, residuals randomly scattered, no systematic pattern.

subsections.heading: Poor Fit
subsections.content: R² < 0.90, systematic residual patterns (curvature, trends), or failed convergence.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.content: Low R² may indicate: incorrect model, noise/artifacts, incomplete reaction, or signal below detection limit. Check the curve browser for visual inspection.

why_it_matters: Accurate parameter estimates are essential for meaningful fold change calculations. Poor fits propagate uncertainty through all downstream analyses.
